bug_description: The peak condition uses `>=` instead of `>` for both comparisons (`mid >= left and mid >= right`), causing plateau endpoints and equal-neighbour positions to be classified as peaks. The original contract requires strict local peaks (nums[i] > both neighbours). With `>=`, any element that is greater than or equal to both its neighbours is reported as a peak, which includes the edge of a plateau run (where mid equals one neighbour) — producing false peaks on plateau boundaries and violating the 'Plateaus are not included' guarantee. The bug is non-trivial because the operator change is minimal but changes semantics on any input with repeated adjacent values.

reference_fix.replacement: if mid > left and mid > right:
